title: Update attack path visualizer icons and arrow rotation

requirements.text: Replace the existing user icon with a man icon in the attack path elements section. The icon should follow the same dimension pattern as existing icons (64x64 or 128x128 PNG) and be placed in frontend/public/assets/.

quotes: replace the existing user with a different styled icon, just a man and should be placed under attack path elements

acceptanceCriteria: The user icon in AttackPathToolbar is replaced with a man icon
The icon maintains consistent sizing with other attack path icons
The icon is accessible via the attack path elements toolbar

requirements.text: Add a Multiple Users icon to the attack path elements section in AttackPathToolbar. The icon should be a new draggable element following the same pattern as existing icons.
acceptanceCriteria: A new Multiple Users icon appears in the attack path elements toolbar
The icon can be dragged onto the canvas
The icon is stored and rendered correctly in the diagram state

requirements.text: Add a Multiple Computers icon to the attack path elements section in AttackPathToolbar. The icon should be a new draggable element following the same pattern as existing icons.
acceptanceCriteria: A new Multiple Computers icon appears in the attack path elements toolbar
The icon can be dragged onto the canvas
The icon is stored and rendered correctly in the diagram state

requirements.text: Enable click-and-drag rotation for arrows in the attack path visualizer. Users should be able to click on an arrow and drag to freely rotate it around its connection points.

quotes: able to click and drag to rotate arrows freely

acceptanceCriteria: Arrows can be selected by clicking on them
Selected arrows can be rotated by dragging
Arrow rotation state is preserved in the diagram state
Rotation feels natural and responsive during interaction

requirements.text: Remove the 'Export as PNG' functionality from the attack path visualizer. This includes removing the export button from AttackPathToolbar and any associated export logic.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Export as PNG' button is no longer visible in the toolbar
All related export functionality and dependencies are removed
No console errors or broken references remain

constraints: Do not modify the backend Motoko code
Do not change the white background color of the canvas
Maintain compatibility with existing diagram save/load functionality
Follow the existing icon pattern used in AttackPathIcon.tsx and AttackPathToolbar.tsx
nonGoals: Adding rotation controls for other elements besides arrows
Adding new export formats to replace PNG export
Modifying the connection logic or arrow drawing algorithm

required: Man icon illustration for attack path diagram (man-icon.dim_128x128.png)
Multiple users icon illustration showing group of people (multiple-users-icon.dim_128x128.png)
Multiple computers icon illustration showing multiple computer devices (multiple-computers-icon.dim_128x128.png)
